Format.spec: text(("%m ", state), ((state == 4) ? ! SW_TOGGLE_STATE ("%d ", setting.delay) : ((state == 5) ? ! SW_SET_LEVEL ("%f ", setting.value) : ((state == 6) ? ! SW_INCREASE_LEVEL ("%f ", setting.change) : ((state == 13) ? ! SW_SET_MULTIPLIER ("%f ", setting.multiplier) : ((state == 14) ? ! SW_ENABLE_GROUP ("%d ", setting.group_number) : ((state == 22) ? ! SW_SET_BUTTON ("%d ", setting.button_number) : ((state == 32) ? ! SW_SET_FAN_UP ("%d ", setting.fan_level) : ((state == 50) ? ! SW_SET_ANGLE ("%d ", setting.angle) : ("%f %d", setting.value, scene_number))))))))))

Format.name: SNVT_program_status
Format.spec: text("%m %d %d:%d:%d:%d %m %d/%d/%d %d:%d:%d", state, runtime.day, runtime.hour, runtime.minute, runtime.second, runtime.millisecond, last_error, time_of_last_error.year, time_of_last_error.month, time_of_last_error.day, time_of_last_error.hour, time_of_last_error.minute, time_of_last_error.second)

Format.name: SNVT_program_status#LO
Format.spec: text(("%m %d ", state, runtime.day), time(runtime.hour, runtime.minute, runtime.second, runtime.millisecond), (" %m: ", last_error), date(time_of_last_error.year, time_of_last_error.month, time_of_last_error.day), (" "), time(time_of_last_error.hour, time_of_last_error.minute, time_of_last_error.second))

Format.name: SNVT_program_status#SI
Format.spec: text("%m %dT%d:%d:%d:%d %m %d-%d-%dT%d:%d:%d", state, runtime.day, runtime.hour, runtime.minute, runtime.second, runtime.millisecond, last_error, time_of_last_error.year, time_of_last_error.month, time_of_last_error.day, time_of_last_error.hour, time_of_last_error.minute, time_of_last_error.second)

Format.name: SCPTtimeZone#LO
Format.spec: text(("%d %m ", second_time_offset, type_of_description), time(hour_of_start_DST, minute_of_start_DST, second_of_start_DST), ((type_of_description == 0) ? ((" %d " ,start_DST.G_day_of_start_DST), time(hour_of_end_DST, minute_of_end_DST, second_of_end_DST), (" %d", end_DST.G_day_of_end_DST)) : ((type_of_description == 1) ? ((" %d ", start_DST.J_day_of_start_DST), time(hour_of_end_DST, minute_of_end_DST, second_of_end_DST), (" %d", end_DST.J_day_of_end_DST)) : ((type_of_description == 2) ? ((" %d %d %m ", start_DST.M_start_DST.month_of_start_DST, start_DST.M_start_DST.week_of_start_DST, start_DST.M_start_DST.dateday_of_start_DST), time(hour_of_end_DST,minute_of_end_DST,second_of_end_DST), (" %d %d %m",end_DST.M_end_DST.month_of_end_DST, end_DST.M_end_DST.week_of_end_DST, end_DST.M_end_DST.dateday_of_end_DST)) : ("UNKNOWN")))))

Format.name: SCPTvalueName
Format.spec: text("%c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c %c",name[0], name[1], name[2], name[3], name[4],name[5], name[6], name[7], name[8], name[9],name[10], name[11], name[12], name[13], name[14],name[15], name[16], name[17], name[18], name[19],name[20], name[21])

Format.name: SCPTtimePeriod
Format.spec: text(("%m ", units), ((units == 0) ? ! IOM_MINUTE ("%d ", value.minutes_interval) : ((units == 1) ? ! IOM_HOUR ("%d ", value.hours_interval) : ((units == 2) ? ! IOM_DAY ("%d ", value.hour_of_day) : ((units == 3) ? ! IOM_WEEK ("%d ", value.day_of_week) : ((units == 4) ? ! IOM_MONTH ("%d ", value.date_of_month) : ("UNKNOWN")))))))

Format.name: SCPTname1
Format.spec: text(("%m ", encoding), ((encoding == 0) ? ! CE_UTF_8 ("%s", name) : ("%x %x %x %x %x %x %x %x %x %x %x %x", name[0], name[1], name[2], name[3], name[4], name[5], name[6], name[7], name[8], name[9], name[10], name[11])))

Format.name: SCPTbuttonPressAction
Format.spec: text(("%m ", action), ((action == 1) ? ! BTA_TOGGLE_SCENE ("%d ", setting.scene_number) : ((action == 3) ? ! BTA_RECALL_SCENE ("%d ", setting.scene_number) : ((action == 15) ? ! BTA_ROTATE_OPEN ("d ", setting.angle) : ((action == 16) ? ! BTA_ROTATE_CLOSED ("%d ", setting.angle) : ((action == 17) ? ! BTA_SET_ANGLE ("%d ", setting.angle) : ((action == 20) ? ! BTA_LEARN_SCENE ("%d ", setting.scene_number) : ((action == 34) ? ! BTA_TOGGLE_GROUP ("%d ", setting.group_number) : ((action == 35) ? ! BTA_ENABLE_GROUP ("%d ", setting.group_number) : ((action == 36) ? ! BTA_DISABLE_GROUP ("%d ", setting.group_number) : ((action == 255) ? ! BTA_NUL ("UNKNOWN ") : ("%f ", setting.value))))))))))))

Format.name: SCPTbuttonHoldAction
Format.spec: text(("%m ", action), ((action == 1) ? ! BTA_TOGGLE_SCENE ("%d ", setting.scene_number) : ((action == 3) ? ! BTA_RECALL_SCENE ("%d ", setting.scene_number) : ((action == 15) ? ! BTA_ROTATE_OPEN ("d ", setting.angle) : ((action == 16) ? ! BTA_ROTATE_CLOSED ("%d ", setting.angle) : ((action == 17) ? ! BTA_SET_ANGLE ("%d ", setting.angle) : ((action == 20) ? ! BTA_LEARN_SCENE ("%d ", setting.scene_number) : ((action == 34) ? ! BTA_TOGGLE_GROUP ("%d ", setting.group_number) : ((action == 35) ? ! BTA_ENABLE_GROUP ("%d ", setting.group_number) : ((action == 36) ? ! BTA_DISABLE_GROUP ("%d ", setting.group_number) : ((action == 255) ? ! BTA_NUL ("UNKNOWN ") : ("%f ", setting.value))))))))))))

Format.name: SCPTsceneName
Format.spec: text(("%m ", encoding), ((encoding == 0) ? ! CE_UTF_8 ("%s", name) : ("%x %x %x %x %x %x %x %x %x %x %x %x", name[0], name[1], name[2], name[3], name[4], name[5], name[6], name[7], name[8], name[9], name[10], name[11])))

Format.name: SCPTmonInterval
Format.spec: text("%d %d:%d:%d:%d", day, hour, minute, second, millisecond)

Format.name: SCPTmonInterval#LO
Format.spec: text(("%d ", day), time(hour, minute, second, millisecond))

Format.name: SCPTscanTime
Format.spec: text("%d %d:%d:%d:%d", day, hour, minute, second, millisecond)

Format.name: SCPTscanTime#LO
Format.spec: text(("%d ", day), time(hour, minute, second, millisecond))

Format.name: SCPTdevListEntry
Format.spec: text(("%m", address_type), ((address_type == 1) ? ! DA_SN (subnet-node ID address) ("%d/%d", sn.subnet, sn.node) : ((address_type == 2) ? ! DA_NI (Neuron ID address, with subnet) ("%d/%x-%x-%x-%x-%x-%x", ni.subnet, ni.nid[0], ni.nid[1], ni.nid[2], ni.nid[3], ni.nid[4], ni.nid[5]) : ("UNKNOWN"))))

Format.name: SCPTlogRecord
Format.spec: text(("%d %d %d %d %d %d ", timestamp_type, record_type, multiple_input, fan_in, point_status, data_length), ((timestamp_type == 0) ? ! TS_FULL ("%d %f ", time.timestamp.second, time.timestamp.hundredths) : ((timestamp_type == 1) ? ! TS_OFFSET ("%f ", time.offset_stamp) : ((timestamp_type == 2) ? ! TS_NONE ("NO_TIMESTAMP ") : ("UNKNOWN ")))), ! Other timestamp_type values are invalid or not used ((multiple_input == 1) ? ! Multiple input flag is set ("%d ", member_index) : ("SINGLE_INPUT ")), ((fan_in == 1) ? ! Data source index included ("%d ", data_source_index) : ("NO_FANIN ")), ((record_type == 0) ? ! LR_DATA ("%d ", data.value) : ((record_type == 1) ? ! LR_LOG_STATUS ("%m ", data.log_status) : ((record_type == 2) ? ! LR_TIME_CHANGE ("%d %f ", data.old_time.second, data.old_time.hundredths) : ("UNKNOWN ")))), ! Other record_type values are invalid or not used ((point_status == 1) ? ! Point status value included ("%m ", point_status_value) : ("NO_POINT_STATUS")))

Format.name: SCPTlogFileHeader
Format.spec: text("%d v%d.%d %d %d %d %f %d %f", file_type, major_version_number, minor_version_number, log_number, record_count, start_time.second, start_time.hundredths, end_time.second, end_time.hundredths)

Format.name: SCPTenableStatusMsg
Format.spec: text("%d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d %d", lamp_current_high, lamp_current_low, main_current_high, main_current_low, lamp_voltage_high, lamp_voltage_low, main_voltage_high, main_voltage_low, power_factor_low, OLC_temp_high, power_high, power_low,relay_failure, cap_failure, lamp_failure, ballast_failure, inter_com_failure, exter_com_failure, main_volt_below_spec, lamp_restart_count, fading_ready, ballast_temp_high, digi_in_A, digi_in_B)

Format.name: SCPTprogRevision#LO
Format.spec: text(("v%d.%d.%d (", major_version,minor_version,build_number),date(build_date.year, build_date.month, build_date.day), (" "), time(build_date.hour, build_date.minute, build_date.second), (") "))

Format.name: SCPTprogCmdHistory#LO
Format.spec: text(date(timestamp.year, timestamp.month, timestamp.day), (" "), time(timestamp.hour, timestamp.minute, timestamp.second),(" - %m: %s",command,description))

Format.name: SCPTprogStateHistory#LO
Format.spec: text(date(time_of_state_change.year, time_of_state_change.month, time_of_state_change.day), (" "), time(time_of_state_change.hour, time_of_state_change.minute, time_of_state_change.second),(" - %m", state))

Format.name: SCPTprogErrorHistory#LO
Format.spec: text(date(time_of_error.year, time_of_error.month, time_of_error.day), (" "), time(time_of_error.hour, time_of_error.minute, time_of_error.second),(" - %m", error))

Strings.String: This file is the standard type file, containing Standard Network Variable Types (SNVTs), Standard Configuration Property Types (SCPTs), and the enumeration types that support them.
This file was created and is maintained by LonMark International. Contact us at +1-408-938-5266, at http://www.lonmark.org, or at 550 Meridian Ave, San Jose CA 95126, USA.
This file is the standard FPT file, containing Standard Functional Profile Templates.
This file was created and is maintained by LonMark International. Contact us at +1-408-938-5266, at http://www.lonmark.org, or at 550 Meridian Ave, San Jose CA 95126, USA.
This file is the standard language-dependent resource file, containing LonWorks device resource strings in English localized for the United Kingdom.
This file was created and is maintained by LonMark International. Contact us at +1-408-938-5266, at http://www.lonmark.org, or at 550 Meridian Ave, San Jose CA 95126, USA.
This SNVT is obsolete. Use SNVT_time_stamp instead.
Zero (0) in this field designates a NULL date.
Wide character string with locale code (15 characters max)
This SNVT is obsolete. Use SNVT_elapsed_tm instead.
There can be 127 nodes (1-127) in a subnet.
There can be 255 subnets (1-255) in a domain.
An acknowledged group can have from 0-64 addressees, plus the sender.
There can be 256 groups (0-255) in a domain.
This field specifies whether the message requires authentication.
This field specifies whether the message is to be sent with priority.
Provides descriptive physical location information related to the entire device.
This SNVT is obsolete. Use SNVT_magcard instead.
Each state is a boolean single-bit value.
year, month, day, hour, minute, second
Zero (0) means year not specified. Minus one (-1) represents NULL date.
Linear transformation parameters: multiply by the span-factor, then add the zero-term.
This data item contains data according to the ISO 7811 standard for card stripes.
The value 65535 represents NULL or unknown elapsed time.
The type of this field is dependent on the NV causing the alarm condition.
Credit is positive, debit is negative.
The selector is used to choose which preset.
This field can either be -1 (NULL), 0 (OFF), or 1 (ON).
This field is used when multiple interpolation tables are linked.
The unit is in the alarm state.
The period for updating the feedback output when the actuator position does not match the requested position
The time period that an alarm 1 condition must not exist before it is regarded as a valid cleared alarm
The time period for which alarms are inhibited after an object is enabled or the node is reset
The time period that an alarm 1 condition must exist before it is regarded as a valid alarm
The time period that an alarm 2 condition must exist before it is regarded as a valid alarm
The position or level the sensor should adopt when updates are not received, or at power-on reset, or when overridden
Time to be taken by the actuator to move from one extreme to the other
The alarm high limit against which the value field of the output value is tested for alarm conditions
The alarm 2nd high limit against which the value field of the output value is tested for alarm conditions
The hysteresis level for the value field of the high limit 1 comparison threshold
The hysteresis level for the value field of the high limit 2 comparison threshold
The hysteresis level for the value field of the low limit 1 comparison threshold
The hysteresis level for the value field of the low limit 2 comparison threshold
The time period after the last update in a succession of changes to the input, before the feedback output is updated
This parameter indicates to invert the active polarity, if the value is nonzero (ON).
Provides descriptive physical location information related to the object.
The alarm low limit against which the value field of the output value is tested for alarm conditions
The alarm 2nd low limit against which the value field of the output value is tested for alarm conditions
The maximum time elapsed after the last update before the actuator adopts the default output
The maximum period of time between consecutive transmissions of the current value
The minimum period of time between consecutive transmissions of the current value
The value of this field determines the source of the node's network configuration.
Used in conjunction with Translation table Y to scale and linearise a value
Used in conjunction with Translation table X to scale and linearise a value
The length of time that the load remains energized after a change from ON to OFF has been received
This parameter is used to calibrate the external hardware.
This parameter is used to define the behavior when an override request is received.
The value a sensor should adopt when an object is overridden, and the behavior is "SPECIFIED"
Allows the function of objects within a node to be monitored. Only one may exist on a node.
Requests a particular mode for a particular object in the device
Reports the status of requested object in the device
Synchronize the node's internal real time clock with an external time source
Transmits alarm data for each object on a node whenever alarm occurs or is cleared, and upon request
Requests an operation on a particular file
Reports the status of the last requested file operation
Value used to control the position of the read/write pointer in a file
Address for file directory containing descriptors for configuration parameter files
Indicates whether the node will configure itself, or expects a network manager
Controls the maximum period of time before the object status is transmitted; Zero (0) means disabled
A basic object without feedback, used with any form of sensor
Transmits the value from the sensor after conversion to correct units
Receives preset function feedback information used to synchronize multiple source objects
Transmits the value obtained from the sensor prior to any transformation
Used to program or control the preset of a destination object
Used to calibrate external hardware via a multiplication factor
Used to calibrate external hardware with additive offset after transformation
The maximum value of the output
The minimum value of the output
The maximum period of time before a retransmission of value
The minimum period of time between retransmissions of value
Used to invert active polarity if the hardware sensor is a switch
Used in conjunction with Translation table Y to create a linearisation table
Used in conjunction with Translation table X to create a linearisation table
Used to remotely set the alarm high limit 1
Used to remotely set the alarm high limit 2
Used to remotely set the alarm low limit 1
Used to remotely set the alarm low limit 2
The time period that an alarm 2 condition must not exist before it is regarded as a valid cleared alarm
The hysteresis level for the value field of the alarm high 1 comparison threshold
The hysteresis level for the value field of the alarm high 2 comparison threshold
The hysteresis level for the value field of the alarm low 1 comparison threshold
The hysteresis level for the value field of the alarm low 2 comparison threshold
Time period for which alarms are inhibited after enabling, node reset, or node is put on line
The level the sensor should adopt in certain default conditions
The behaviour of a sensor when an override request is received
Sets the value a sensor should adopt when an object is overridden and behaviour is OV_SPECIFIED
A basic object with feedback, used with any form of sensor
Used to synchronise closed loop sensor objects when connected in multiple relationships
A basic object without feedback, used with any form of actuator
Dictates the desired state of the actuator, determined by the specific application
Used to program or control the preset function
Present position of the actuator, can be used as part of a control loop and for monitoring purposes

Used in spaces that can be divided into smaller sections. Object passes data to the area next to it if the space is open (no partitioning). If space is divided, then light-switch data is not passed to the other side of the separating wall.

Provides the scene output to the scene controllers on the primary side of the partition wall
The valid range of the input is open (state ON) and closed (state OFF). Other enumerations of the state are discarded; value field has no effect.

Reads in data from the scene panels and other sensors of the secondary side of the partition wall

Provides SNVT_setting type output to the scene controllers on the secondary side of the partition wall
Sends events over the network that are based on real time
Provides a time- and date-related event for the controller
Provides real-time-related operational-mode information for the controller
Used to control the room temperature by controlling one heat and one cool actuator output, or a single actuator for heat and cool, and a fan with multiple fan-speed stages

Provides other devices the state of an EnergyHoldOff device that is hardwired to the controller
Combines operating mode, capacity of heating/cooling used, and an indication if any alarms are present in the object
The value 1 implies one output valve (two pipe system) and the value 2 implies two output valves (four pipe system).

A value of 0 indicates primary heating is not saturated. A value of 1 indicates primary heating is saturated.
A value of 0 indicates secondary heating is not saturated. A value of 1 indicates secondary heating is saturated.
A value of 0 indicates primary cooling is not saturated. A value of 1 indicates primary cooling is saturated.
A value of 0 indicates secondary cooling is not saturated. A value of 1 indicates secondary cooling is saturated.
A value of 0 indicates primary duct is not saturated (starved). A value of 1 indicates primary duct is saturated (starved).
A value of 0 indicates secondary duct is not saturated (starved). A value of 1 indicates secondary duct is saturated (starved).
Four manufacturer-defined bits -- please see product documentation for proper interpretation of these bits
Mode that a device has to be brought to when a safety external request state is pending
Mode that a device has to be brought to when an emergency request state is pending

Role of a device in a sluice-lock connection. A sluice-lock is an interlock mechanism between two entry/exit devices, or a sluice manager and several entry/exit devices, to ensure that only one single entry/exit device is opened at any point in time.
Unit minimum air flow for dual duct VAV Terminal units
Unit maximum airflow for dual duct VAV Terminal units

Used to inform the controller if it is in a single or dual duct system
Minimum airflow setpoint of a single duct, or the unit minimum airflow setpoint of a dual duct VAV terminal when using a unit (local) heating source

Identifies the subsystem containing the device. The subsystem may be a simple location name or a hierarchical subsystem name. If a hierarchical subsystem name is specified, the subsystem hierarchy components must be separated by periods("."). Periods must not otherwise be used. Other characters that cannot be used in a subsystem name are the backslash ("\"), colon (":"), forward slash ("/"), or double-quote characters. For very large networks, subsystem numbers may be used instead of subsystem names, for example: "1.2.29".
Measured space CO2 or VOC levels in PPM
Dynamic minimum position setpoint for an outdoor air damper. When valid it will supercede nciOAMinPos.
Dynamic minimum cooling air flow setpoint for single or dual duct VAV terminal units. When valid it will supercede nciMinFlow.

Dynamic minimum heating air flow setpoint for single or dual duct VAV terminal units. When valid it will supercede nciMinFlowHeat.
The controller will add this input with nciFlowOffset to derive the active flow setpoint
When used with nvoTerminalLoad from another controller can be used to coordinate master/salve operation
The temperature of the combined return and fresh airstreams in an AHU before they reach the water coils
Effective air flow heat setpoint output
The hot or ventilation duct flow setpoint of a dual duct unit.
Flow control damper heat setpoint output

Air flow of a hot or ventilation deck of a dual duct VAV terminal
Indicates whether the control algorithm capacity limits, or end device physical limits, have been reached
Specifies fan operation during occupied and occupied standby. Fan operation during unoccupied is manufacturer defined
Unit maximum air flow for dual duct VAV Terminal units

Position of car to nearest floor, starting with 1. 0 means car position unknown.
An Entry / Exit device is a door, lock, sluice, or something that allows and prohibits entry of a physical unit in to and out of an area.

Whether the mechanical equipment being subject to locking is in lockable position or not. Typically, this can be an open or closed door.
Alarm setting when the device has been unable to perform a requested command for more than the nciPreAlarmDly time
opens the device in any state but EM_BLOCKED or EM_MANUAL. After the open command is taken away, the device goes back to its previous state.

The maximum period of time that may expire with no updates on the associated input network variables before the object goes into heartbeat failure mode. A zero value disables.

Instructs the gong to sound an 'UP' indication

A value of 0 in a field indicates that the resource associated with that field has not saturated or reached an end stop before attaining the required setpoint. A value of 1 indicates that the resource associated with that field has saturated or reached an end stop without attaining the required setpoint.
Master-slave control for sluice. A sluice is built with two or more entry/exit devices with interlock capability.
Used to report alarm status for a functional block or device. Replaces SNVT_alarm
Alarm condition reported by this update
Priority level of the alarm reported by this update
Alarm time in seconds since 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z (the 0 hour of 1 January 2000, Coordinated Universal Time, UTC)
Alarm time in milliseconds since the second specified by the alarm_time field

Sequence number for this update. Incremented by one for each update from an alarm source. Wraps to zero after reaching 255. An alarm receiver can use the sequence number to detect missed alarm messages.
Alarm description with NUL terminator. The terminator is not required if the description requires 22 characters. May include a reference to a language string, delimited by a 0x80 value.
Determines the interpretation of the network-variable content
Union of device status for various devices

Reports the status of a schedule
Number of days until this schedule will be active. Positive if a schedule is inactive; zero or negative if a schedule is active
Number of days until this schedule will be inactive. Positive if a schedule is active; zero or negative if a schedule is inactive
Nul-terminated schedule name. The nul terminator is not required if the name is 22 characters.
Index from scheduler that selects entry in SCPTvalueDefinition array, or is a direct value output
Time period during which a functional block is effective.
Starting date of the effective period
Ending date of the effective period
Ending year of the effective period

Ending day of the effective period
A range of dates with an optional qualifier that specifies when a schedule is active
Identifies a temporary schedule. Temporary schedules are deleted at the end of the day that they are active
Months within the dates specified by the start and end dates
Days within the dates specified by the start and end dates
Index into a schedule or schedule name array
Describes the attributes of a daily schedule definition
Specifies the priority for this schedule. Low priority values specify high priority, and high priority values specify low priority. Zero (0) is the highest priority and 255 is the lowest
Identifies the starting entry of a list of time-value events in a SCPTscheduleTimeValue array. The end of the list is identified by the terminator field in the SCPTscheduleTime entry
Specifies the time and value for a scheduled event
Identifies the last entry in a time-value list
Hours since midnight for a scheduled event
Minute within the hour for a scheduled event
Specifies the value to output when a time-value pair is active. The value must be mapped to a value that matches the type of the output network variable
Used to create an array of output values to be used for a schedule. A schedule time-value event specifies a value as an index into a SCPTvalueDefinition array
Used to create an array of value names for each of the values defined in a SCPTvalueDefinition array
Nul-terminated name string of up to 22 characters. The nul terminator is not required if the string is 22 characters.
Identifies a schedule to be active for each day of the week
Used to create an array of names for schedules defined by a SCPTschedule array or a SCPTscheduleDates array.
Transmits alarm data for each functional block on a device whenever an alarm occurs or is cleared, and upon request. Replaces nvoAlarm
Reports the status of a schedule. Optional input for the node object for devices with Scheduler functional blocks
Requests an update for all defined exceptions via the nviDateEvent input. Required output from the node object for devices with Scheduler functional blocks
For use with centrifugal, absorption, reciprocating, and other models of chillers
Desired temperature of leaving chilled water
Present capacity limit setting of chiller
Provides descriptive physical location information related to the object
Provides the primary force to distribute and circulate hot and chilled water in a variety of space-temperature and air-conditioning systems.
Total running time for the pump in hours. After 65535 hours, the counter starts again at zero.
Total energy consumption of the pump
Standardised interface for identifying active and inactive schedules based on dates. Schedules may become active on a particular date, on a range of dates, or on a repeating interval
Requests an update on the status of all defined schedules
Time period during which the calendar is effective
Behaviour of the nvoDateEvent output when an override request is received for the calendar
The value the nvoDateEvent output should adopt when it is overridden and the value of Override Behaviour is OV_SPECIFIED
Minimum period of time between automatic transmissions of the nvoDateEvent output. This throttles the series of outputs that occur whenever schedules are updated via nvoDateEvent
Standardised interface for describing daily schedules. Schedules may become active on specified days of the week, or based on active schedules defined by a Calendar functional block
Indicates the current value of the schedule. The output is determined by the current time as well as the currently active schedules
Enables the scheduler. The scheduler is enabled when the state value is one (1) and the level value is greater than zero (0)

Specifies the nvoPresentValue output when no schedules are active, or when an override request is received for the functional block. The override behaviour may be defined by the optional Override Behavior and Override Value configuration properties

Specifies a delay from the scheduled time. This allows multiple outputs within a Scheduler functional block, or multiple Scheduler functional blocks on a device, to share a common schedule but stagger on and off times to reduce peak load
Maximum length of a type that may be assigned to the nvoPresentValue network variable
Assigns the network variable type for the nvoPresentValue network variable

Sets the value the nvoPresentValue output should adopt when it is overridden and the value of Override Behaviour is OV_SPECIFIED
Used to create an array of schedule names
nciSetPnts is not defined in the profile due to its type inheriting from the Principal NV of the profile. No mandatory NVs are of SNVT_temp_setpt, and therefore it cannot inherit the correct type
This value must be input in °C
An Identifier Sensor can be any kind of device used to identify a person who wants to access a controlled point/area.
A Modem Controller is used to control the functions of a data modem (POTS, ISDN, etc.).
A Telephone Directory is used to store and retreive arrays of ASCII strings that are characterized as telephone numbers (including characters used for control) used in dialing a data modem.
Telephone-number entry index (0-to-maximum), where maximum is less than or equal to 255

An ATS is a piece of electrical equipment that is used to monitor and automatically connect the best of two power sources to an electrical load. Normally at least one of the two power sources is a generator set that is directed by the ATS to start when the primary power source has failed
Allows an external node to test the ATS operation
Allows the ATS to start and stop the alternate or emergency power source
Indicates the availability of the normal power source (GenSet/Source 1)
Indicates the availability of the emergency power source (GenSet/Source 2)
Indicates the switch position of the ATS
Resets or attempts to clear an ATS fault
Sheds the electrical load of the ATS
Inhibits the ATS from automatically transferring the load to the emergency power source (Source 2), when Source 2 becomes available
Inhibits the ATS from automatically transferring the load back to the normal power source (Source 1), when Source 1 becomes available
Overrides a transfer or retransfer time delay, or transfer or retransfer inhibit
Allows the ATS to start and stop the Source 1
Reports the presence of an ATS fault
Line current(s) of the electrical load
Power factor of the electrical load
Real power of the electrical load as a floating type ( in Watts)
Maximum send time to be used across NVs 14-22.

This input NV controls the UFI object
This output NV reflects the operational conditition of the initiator device

This output NV reflects the operational condition of the indicator device
Reflects the actuator value of nviActuDrive. Used to synchronise source objects in multiple relationships.
This input NV receives requests intended to perform smoke detector operations, initiated from operator
This outpur NV transmits fire information for use by simple Indicators
This output NV transmitted initiator trouble information for use by simple Indicators
This input NV controls the (actuator) position for smoke control devices
This input network variable receives the status (ON or OFF) request (command) for an audible indicating device. It can be bound to the nvoAlarm network variable(s) of initiating device(s)
This output network variable transmits the feedback status (ON or OFF) of indicating device.
This input network variable receives the status (ON or OFF) request (command) for an Visible indicating device. It can be bound to the nvoAlarm network variable(s) of initiating device(s)
This output network variable transmits the feedback status (ON or OFF) of indicating evice.
start and stop the generator set
reset or clear a generator set fault
running at rated speed and voltage and is ready to accept load
report the presence of a generator set fault
report state of National Fire Protection Agency (USA) genset faults (NFPA §110)

The nvoDefrostState indicates the current state of the defrost object.
nviTemperature1 is to be assigned to inlet or outlet of the evaporator as required. An error on the sensor is indicated with a value outside the valid range.
The nviTemperature2 is to be assigned to inlet or outlet of the evaporator as required. An error on the sensor is indicated with a value outside the valid range.
If synchronised defrost is selected as the control strategy, this network variable indicates that the defrost object can begin the after defrost sequence.
nviDefrostTerminate can be used as an input from some external sensor e.g. ice sensor to indicate the level of defrosting.
The nviHumidity can be used to provide humidity information for the defrost algorithm.
The current opening degree of the valve, in percent of fully open.
The current state of the evaporator object
These values indicate the current evaporator inlet (liquid line) temperature. The input can be used if the sensor is external to the evaporator object.
These values indicate the current evaporator inlet (liquid line) temperature. The output can be used if the sensor is internal to the evaporator object.
These values indicate the current evaporator outlet (suction line) temperature. The input can be used if the sensor is external to the vaporator object.
These values indicate the current evaporator outlet (suction line) temperature. The output can be used if the sensor is internal to the evaporator object.
The nviForcedValve is used to force the valve to a given opening degree. The evaporator object will stay in this forced mode as long as SNVT_switch.state equals TRUE.

The nvoSuperHeatTemp indicates the true evaporator super heat temperature. This variable should be used only when both pressure & temperature are used for calculation. If only temperatures are used then the delta temperature output should be used
This NV indicates the current state of the Thermostat Object. There are currently three different control methods supported by the Thermostat Object (See PDF)
The nvoSuperHeatRef indicates the current target evaporator super heat temperature.
This NV indicates the current state of the defrost object.
The nviSuperHeatRef is an override input for the target super heat reference. This input should be used when the object is in override.
This NV is the calculated case air temperature. An error on the sensor is indicated with the error value for SNVT_temp_p (0x7fff).
This NV indicates the current cut out limit used by the thermostat object in its agorithms.
This NV indicates the value to be added to the nviCutoutTemp to get the thermostat cut in limit if cut in / out control is selected.
This NV indicates the inferred evaporator super heat temperature. This variable should be used when pressure is not taken into account in the calculation.
The pressure of the refrigerant in the liquid (evaporator feed) line.
The input variable would be included on nodes without the hardware interface to read a pressure sensor, whereas the output variable would be included on nodes with pressure sensor hardware.
This NV is to be assigned to discharge or return air as required. An error on the sensor is indicated with the error value for SNVT_temp_p (0x7fff).
The thermostat object can be configured to use this network variable as a day/night indicator. ST_OFF indicates day and ST_ON indicates night.
The nvoAlarmAirTemp indicates the current air temperature used by the alarm section of the thermostat object.
The nvoDischargeTemp indicates the current evaporator discharge air temperature used by the thermostat object.
The nvoReturnTemp indicates the current evaporator return air temperature used by the thermostat object.
This output can be used to drive a refrigeration valve or compressor.
This output variable reports the pressure of the sensor using the Kilo Pascal pressure SNVT_press.
This output variable reports the pressure of the sensor using the Floating point pressure SNVT.
This output variable reports the pressure of the sensor using the high precision pressure SNVT_press_p.
This output network variable provides the state of the frost detector.
This output network variable provides the state of the rain detector.
This network variable contains the current value of the register with a time stamp and status bits all contained within one variab
This input network variable controls which history value is shown on the output network variable side via the network variables voHistVal and nvoHistTime.
This input network variable controls, according to the time, which history value is shown on the output network variable side via the network variables nvoHistVal and nvoHistTime.
A value can be sent to the register object via the network using this network variable

The register can be activated or deactivated by using this variable. When a register is activated the register object measures into the register. If the register is deactivated it does not measure but it is read/write-able.

This input network variable controls the ending of a billing period. When the billing period is terminated, the current register value will be transferred to the next history register. The series register cannot use this roperty.
This output network variable contains the register object’s historical value of a chosen time or history period. The output value is selected using the nviHistChoice or nviHistTime variables.
This output network variable contains a billing period end time. The output value is selected using the nviHistChoice and nviHistTime variables.
This output network variable contains the length of the measuring period. If used, the register object measures series or maximum value data. Otherwise the value is zero.
This input network variable triggers a group or pattern of lighting circuits.
This output network variable provides current status of any of the possible 255 groups or patterns programmed into the controller.
This input network variable provides the functions required for programming groups, patterns and other lighting behaviors into the controller.
This output network variable provides information on current LPC configuation.
This input network variable is used to connect an external space temperature sensor to the node.
This input network variable is used to allow the temperature setpoint for the occupied and standby mode to be changed via the network.
This output network variable is used to send the value of the controlled space temperature (either from a locally connected space temperature sensor or from the nviSpaceTemp input) to other nodes.
This output network variable is available to report the object status.
This network variable input is used to coordinate the RTU Device with any supervisory controller or intelligent human interface device.
This input network variable is used to command the RTU object into different occupancy modes. It is typically set by a supervisory node.
This input network variable is used to shift the temperature setpoint via the network by adding nviSetPtOffset to the current setpoint.
This input network variable is the current outside air temperature.
This input network variable is the current outside air relative humidity.
The output network variable is used to inform the effective setpoint temperature when the setpoint is changed by nciSetPnts, nviSetPoint, nviSetPointOffset or local means.
This output network variable is the current outside air temperature.
This output network variable is the current outside air relative humidity.
This output network variable is the CO2 measurement.
This input network variable measures the space relative humidity.
This input network variable measures the CO2 levels in PPM.
This input network variable is used to command the RTU object into different emergency modes.
This input network variable is used to allow the temperature setpoint for the occupied and standby mode being changed via the network.
This output network variable reflects the current position of the heat actuator and can be used as part of a control loop and for monitoring purposes.
This output network variable reflects the current position of the cool actuator and can be used as part of a control loop and for monitoring purposes.
This output network variable is used to send the value of a locally connected space temperature sensor to other nodes. It is mandatory to the profile.
This input network variable is used to command the thermostat object into different occupancy modes.
This network variable input is used to coordinate the thermostat object with any supervisory controller providing the supply energy, e.g. hot or cold water.
This input network variable is used to shift the temperature setpoint via network by adding nviSetPtOffset to the current setpoint.
This input is from a device such as a door/window contact sensor.
This output network variable reflects the current heat/cool energy demand of the Thermostat Device which is typically bound to an energy providing node.
The output network variable is used to report the effective setpoint temperature when the setpoint is changed through nciSetPnts, nviSetPoint, nviSetPtOffset or by local means.
This output network variable reflects the current fan speed of a multi-speed (n-speed) fan. It can be used as part of a control loop and for monitoring purposes.

This input network variable is used to connect an external space temperature sensor to the node.

This input network variable is used to allow the temperature setpoints for the occupied and standby modes to be changed via the network.

This input is used to stop heating and cooling while allowing the unit to protect the space from temperature extremes.

This input network variable is used to indicate the temperature of the air or water being supplied to the unit for cooling capacity.

This output network variable can used to indicate the current power consumption of the unit.

This output network variable reflects the current level of the secondary heat output or can be used to control a remote secondary heat source.
This output network variable reflects the current level of the primary mechanical cooling output or can be used to control a remote mechanical cooling source.

Scene, setting, occupancy, and/or request input to the lamp.

Level multiplier feedback. Reset to one after 60 minutes.
Name of the object that the CP applies to. This name can be used by optional user interface applications or display devices.
Input value when updates are not received, at power-on reset, or when overridden.
Major version number for the associated functional block. See also cpFbMinVer.
Minor version number for the associated functional block. See also cpFbMajVer.
Scene table defining the minimum required scene definition entries. May be used in combination with cpSceneTiming.

Scene timing definition used to supplement a scene table created with cpScene. This CP array defines the optional scene table entries for the ISI profiles. When used, it must be used in combination with a cpScene array.

Part 2 of the name of the functional block to be used by optional user interface applications. Must be used with SCPTname1 and may optionally be used with SCPTname3. This part is concatenated after part 1, and may optionally be followed by part 3. Must be implemented as a configuration network variable.

Part 3 of the name of the functional block to be used by optional user interface applications. Must be used with SCPTname1 and SCPTname2. This part, if present, is concatenated with parts 1 and 2. Must be implemented as a configuration network variable.

The maximum period of time between consecutive transmissions of the current value. If this value is set to the invalid value, the heartbeat interval will be two minutes when occupied and 20 minutes when unoccupied--this is the default behavior. Must be implemented as a configuration network variable.
Name for a scene to be used by optional user interface applications. Used to create an array that supplements a scene table created with a SCPTscene array.
Selected state, scene, and setting output. Also reports power consumption by the keypad, which may be measured or estimated.
Optional feedback input that may be used to synchronize the keypad state to other devices such as other keypads and lamps.

Part 1 of the name of the functional block to be used by optional user interface applications. May optionally used with SCPTname2 and SCPTname3. Must be implemented as a configuration network variable.
Button action definition used to create a button pressed action array, with an entry per button. This SCPT defines the minimum entries required by the ISI profiles.
Button color configuration for on and off states of a button. May be used to create an array that is used with a SCPTbuttonAction array to specify keypad button behavior.
Output value at power-on reset or when overridden
Time between updates when a button is held down. The updates themselves may be throttled by the application or a SCPTminSendTime CP. Used to create an array used with a SCPTbuttonAction CP array.
Reports the state, scene, and setting selected on a keypad. A keypad may consist of one or more push buttons or toggle switches, each optionally including a lamp indicator such as an LED.

Controls the illumination level of a lamp or state of an appliance. Also reports lamp or appliance status with integrated scene control. This profile provides additional required interfaces over the SFPTlampActuator profile to simplify use in self-installed networks.

Provides a monitoring input for use by a controller monitoring an ISI network. The input uses automatic enrollment to provide an easy way for a controller and monitored device to work together to support event-driven updates, providing a method that minimizes network transactions and provides easy set up for devices that support automatic enrollment.

This configuration property defines the interval over which statistics are collected and averages are calculated.

If this limit is implemented and is enabled via nciBandUtlLimHighEnable, if the alarm type was not AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and the value for the last interval was greater than nciBandUtlLimHigh, then the alarm type becomes AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and nvoChnlAlarm will be set to True.If the alarm type was AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and the value drops below nciBandUtlLimHigh minus nciBandUtlHystHigh, then the alarm type becomes AL_HIGH_LMT_CLR_1 and nvoChnlAlarm will be set to False. The hysteresis value is used only to subtract from the limit value.
Hysteresis value for the high alarm nciBandUtlLimHigh.

The low alarm limit can be useful to detect that, for instance, a certain level of background heartbeat traffic expected on a channel has slowed or stopped-which indicates a power, channel wiring, or other problem. If the low alarm limit is enabled with nciBandUtlLimLowEnable, also set the nciBandUtlLimLow property to a non-zero value that is slightly below the expected level of background heartbeat traffic. If the alarm type was not AL_LOW_LMT_ALM_1 and the value is now less than nciBandUtlLimLow, then the alarm type becomes AL_LOW_LMT_ALM_1 and nvoChnlAlarm will be set to True.If the alarm type was AL_LOW_LMT_ALM_1 and the value rises above nciBandUtlLimLow plus nciBandUtlHystLow, then the alarm type becomes AL_LOW_LMT_CLR_1 and nvoChnlAlarm will be set to False. The hysteresis value is used only to add to the limit value.
Hysteresis value for the low alarm nciBandUtlLimLow.

If this limit is implemented and is enabled via nciCrcErrLimHighEnable, if the alarm type was not AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and the value for the last interval was greater than nciCrcErrLimHigh, then the alarm type becomes AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and nvoChnlAlarm will be set to True.If the alarm type was AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and the value drops below nciCrcErrLimHigh minus nciCrcErrHystHigh, then the alarm type becomes AL_HIGH_LMT_CLR_1 and nvoChnlAlarm will be set to False. The hysteresis value is used only to subtract from the limit value.
CRC error rate high limit enable
CRC error rate high limit hysteresis
Hysteresis value for the high alarm nciCrcErrLimHigh.

If this limit is implemented and is enabled via nciMissedLimHighEnable, if the alarm type was not AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and the value for the last interval was greater than nciMissedLimHigh, then the alarm type becomes AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and nvoChnlAlarm will be set to True.If the alarm type was AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and the value drops below nciMissedLimHigh minus nciMissedHystHigh, then the alarm type becomes AL_HIGH_LMT_CLR_1 and nvoChnlAlarm will be set to False. The hysteresis value is used only to subtract from the limit value.
Missed packet rate high limit enable
Missed packet rate high limit hysteresis
Hysteresis value for the high alarm nciMissedLimHigh.

If this limit is implemented and is enabled via nciMisPreLimHighEnable, if the alarm type was not AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and the value for the last interval was greater than nciMisPreLimHigh, then the alarm type becomes AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and nvoChnlAlarm will be set to True.If the alarm type was AL_HIGH_LMT_ALM_1 and the value drops below nciMisPreLimHigh minus nciMisPreHystHigh, then the alarm type becomes AL_HIGH_LMT_CLR_1 and nvoChnlAlarm will be set to False. The hysteresis value is used only to subtract from the limit value.
Missed preamble rate high limit enable
Missed preamble rate high limit hysteresis
Hysteresis value for the high alarm nciMisPreLimHigh.
Determines, whether link power detection is enabled. If yes, nvoLinkPower indicates existence of link power voltage.

A device (typically an EN 14908 -based router) which is capable of channel continuity monitoring has two connectors , which are connected to both ends of a LonWorks bus. The connectors are referred to as channel side A and channel side B. If the bus is interrupted between those two connectors (e.g. due to a cable break), the device must have some means to detect this condition. The Channel Continuity Monitor object is used to publish the current state of the channel (intact, fault) using network variables and the alarming mechanism of the Node Object.
Signals if the channel is OK or interrupted.

A device monitor functional profile is used to monitor the health state of other devices in the network by periodically polling them using query status network diagnostic messages. If a device goes (soft) offline or is no longer reachable over the network, an alarm is generated and output network variables can be used to determine the state of all monitored nodes.
Shows which devices can be reached via side A.
Shows which devices can be reached via side B
Duration in which all devices are being queried.
Device list entry containing the address of the device to be monitored.
Human readable description for an entry in the device list.

Controls the position and rotation angle of a sunblind. Also reports sunblind status with integrated scene control. This profile provides additional required interfaces over the SFPTsunblindActuator profile to simplify use in self-installed networks.

Reports the occupancy state for a zone. Occupancy is reported when detected. Feedback from other connected occupancy sensors within the zone is monitored--the unoccupied state is only propagated if no connected occupancy sensors are reporting occupancy.

Reports the occupancy state for a zone. Occupancy is reported when detected. Feedback from other connected occupancy sensors within the zone is monitored--the unoccupied state is only propagated if no connected occupancy sensors are reporting occupancy.
Used to synchronise multiple occupancy sensors within a zone

Controls test mode. Set to SW_SET_ON to enable test mode--turning off the hold time and ignoring the feedback input; set to SW_SET_OCCUPIED to force the output to occupied; set to SW_SET_UNOCCUPIED to force output to unoccupied; set to SW_SET_OFF to enable normal operation. Default is SW_SET_OFF.

The interval after a change to the occupied state that the occupancy sensor input is ignored. Must be implemented as a configuration network variable.

Hold time for the nvoSwitch occupied state after there is no occupancy detected. The hold time timer is retriggered each time the sensor reports the area as occupied. Must be implemented as a configuration network variable.

The maximum period of time between consecutive transmissions of the current value. If this value is set to the invalid value, the heartbeat interval will be two minutes when occupied and 20 minutes when unoccupied--this is the default behavior. Must be implemented as a configuration network variable.
The time period after a change in the calculated state from occupied to unoccupied before the unoccupied state is reported. Must be implemented as a configuration network variable.

Reports the current status of a data log. Updated based on the cpLogNotificationThreshold value. Reports status only; alarms reported via Node Object nvoAlarm2 output. Required if the Node Object does not include an nvoLogStat output.
State of the selected data log.
The log number of the reported data log. Logs are numbered from 1 to number_of_logs.
The percent of maximum records in the selected data log.
Number of records in the selected data log. A record is a logged value and any associated data such as a timestamp.
Number of bytes in the selected data log.
Total records collected in the selected data log since the data log was created. Wraps to 0 on overflow.
The number of log records collected since the last notificiation.
Timestamp of the most recently collected data point.
Timestamp of the most recently collected data point in the previous update to the log status.
Timestamp with hundredths of a second resolution.
Time in seconds since 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z (the 0 hour of 1 January 2000, Coordinated Universal Time)
Hundredths portion of a the timestamp.

Requests a data log to be transferred via FTP. Must be followed by a stanard FTP request to get the data log file. Required on devices implementing the Data Logger functional profile that support data log transfer via FTP.
The log number of the data log to be transferred. Logs are numbered from 1 to number_of_logs.
The maximum number of log records to be transferred.
Timestamp of first record to be transferred. If no records exist with this timestamp, the first record with a timestamp after this timestamp is the starting record.
Timestamp of last record to be transferred. If no records exist with this timestamp, the last record with a timestamp before this timestamp is the ending record.
Reports the status of a data log file transfer using FTP. Required on devices implementing the Data Logger functional profile that support data log transfer via FTP.
Subnet ID of the device that requested the current log file transfer. Invalid if a file transfer is not active.
Node ID of the device that requested the current log file transfer. Invalid if a file transfer is not active.
Log number for the log file currently being transferred via FTP. Invalid if none.
Data log file transfer percent complete.
Percent of the current data log file transfer that has been completed. Invalid if none.
Requests the current status of a data log. Status is reported by a SNVT_log_status output.
Specifies the total capacity of all data logs on a device. The size of each data log is specified by its cpLogSize value. The value is specified in bytes.
Specifies the percentage change in log level required to trigger an update to the Data Log Status (nvoStatus) output.
Specifies the method used to store data in a data log.
Enables fan-in of multiple data sources. When True, the application examines the source address of each input value and uses it to determine the data source of the update.
Enables time stamping of each data value. When True, the data logger includes a timestamp of the receipt time for each value received by the data logger.

Enables logging of data greater or equal to the specified value. All other data is ignored, with the exception that data that is less than or equal to a valid SCPTlogLowLimit value is also logged. All data is logged if the SCPTlogEnableHighLimit and SCPTlogEnableLowLimit values are both invalid.

Enables logging of data less than or equal to the specified value. All other data is ignored, with the exception that data that is greater than or equal to a valid SCPTlogHighLimit value is also logged. All data is logged if the SCPTlogEnableHighLimit and SCPTlogEnableLowLimit values are both invalid.
Specifies the maximum number of data sources that may be connected to a network variable. The functional block determines data sources by examining the source address of each update.

Minimum amount of time between logged values. This is used to throttle data entry into a data log. When a data value is logged, a subsequent update to the data value is not logged until the time specified by this value has elapsed. If additional updates are received during this time, the older values are discarded and are not stored in the data log. Time of receipt is ignored if the value of this configuration property is zero or invalid.

Minimum amount of time between logged values. This is used to throttle data entry into a data log. When a data value is logged, a subsequent update to the data value is not logged until the time specified by this value has elapsed. If additional updates are received during this time, the older values are discarded and are not stored in the data log. Time of receipt is ignored if the value of this configuration property is zero or invalid.

Specifies the poll rate for each data source. When this value is greater than zero, the functional block polls each of the data sources identified in the source address array at the rate specified by this value.
Specifies a source address or element of an array of source addresses for and input to a functional block.
Set to 0. Applications must ignore this bit.
EN 14908-1 node ID. The value 0 is invalid.
Documents the format of a data log record. Not used as a CP. Data logs have variable sized records--unused fields are not present.
Specifies whether or not a timestamp is included, and if it is incljuded, specifies the format of the timestamp. Contents defined by timestamp_t.
Specifies the format of a data log record. Contents defined by log_record_t.
Set to 1 if the functional block has multiple input NVs.
Set to one if this input NV receives data from multiple data sources.
Set to one if this data log record includes point status information.
Number of bytes in the data portion of a data log record. Set to 0 if the record does not contain a data value.
Full timestamp. Only present if timestamp_type is TS_FULL.
Offset since last full timestamp. Only present if timestamp_type is TS_OFFSET.
Functional block member number for the network variable input that received this update. Only present if mulitple_input is 1.
Index into the cpSourceAddress array. Only present if fan_in is 1.
Data field for a data log record. Contents depend on record_type value. Unused bytes are not included.
Changed value for data log status. Only present if record_type is LR_LOG_STATUS.
Data log value. Size depends on size logged value and is defined by data_length. Only present if record_type is LR_DATA.
Previous time after a time change. Only present if record_type is LR_TIME_CHANGE.
Optional status for a data point value. Only present if point_status is 1.
Describes contents of a data log.
Data log file format major version number.
Index of the data log functional block that received this update.
Number of records in data log.
Specifies the log level required to trigger an alarm condition for the data logger.
Data log access request message format. Not used as a CP. This request has a variable size--the timestamp field is only included with the get next record request.
Data log access protocol version number. Currently must be 1.
The log number of the data log to be accessed. Logs are numbered from 1 to number_of_logs.
Timestamp of last record fetched. The first record with a timestamp after this timestamp is returned. If this field is invalid, the first record in the data log is returned.
Timestamp of last record fetched. The first record with a timestamp after this timestamp is returned. If this field is invalid, the first record in the data log is returned.
Data log access response message format. Not used as a CP. This response has a variable size--unused fields are not included.
Log record. See UCPTlogRecord for definition. This is a variable size field. Returned with LRC_SUCCESS responses.
Closest data log access protocol version.
Closest data log access protocol version number supported by this device. Returned for LRC_VER_MISMATCH responses.
Number of data logs in the device. Returned for LRC_BAD_LOG_INDEX responses.
Reset all channel statistic counters by setting nviClearStat to {100, ON} and back to {0, OFF}. Required on devices implementing the Channelmonitor
Time since the last reboot of the device

Requests the current status of a data log. Status is reported by the nvoLogStat output. Required on a device containing a Node Object with an nvoLogStat output if the device implements multiple Data Logger functional blocks.
Enables data collection for the data log. Data collection is enabled when the state value is one (1). The level value is not used.
Receives data to be collected in the data log. Optional because data sources may be internal to the device.
Clears the contents of the data log. The data log may also be cleared by a Node Object request.

Transmits alarm data to a monitoring device. The alarm type is set to AL_NO_CONDITION if the level set by cpLogAlarmThreshold is not reached and to AL_ALM_CONDITION if the level has been reached or exceeded. The priority is set to PR_10.
Major version number for the data logger implementation. This value is incremented when the network interface for the functional block changes.

Minor version number for the data logger implementation. This value is incremented when the functional block implementation changes without changing the functional block network interface. The minor version is reset to zero and the cpObjMajVer value is incremented when the functional block network interface changes.

Maximum period of time that expires before the Data Logger functional block automatically transmits the current value of the nvoStatus output network variable. This provides a heartbeat output that can be used by destination functional blocks to ensure that the functional block is still healthy.
Maximum length of a type that may be assigned to the nviDataValue input. Also requires implementation of the cpNvType configuration property.

Longitude is given as an angular measurement ranging from 0° at the prime meridian to +180° eastward and -180° westward
Latitude is given as an angular measurement ranging from 0° at the equator to +90° northward and -90° southward
Time when the luminare will be switched ON in case of communication failure
Time when the luminare will be switched ON in case of communication failure
Time when the luminare will be switched OFF in case of communication failure
Time when the luminare will be switched OFF in case of communication failure
MIN/MAX values for the status report are set here
Main voltage below performance specification (DALI Bit 1)
Lamp restart retry counter / cycling failure (DALI Bit 2)
The desired state (on/off) and value (%) of the lamp bulb
The method used to reset or initialize the run-hours count
The method used to reset or initialize the electricity-consumption total
The actual state (on/off) and value (%) of the lamp bulb
The data related to the internal status conditions of the lamp controller
The measured values from in and around the environment of the fixture, including mains/lamp voltages, mains/lamp currents, lamp temperature, consumed power, and power factor
The On/Off schedule to be used as a default
The maximum wattage of the installed bulbs.
Determines which status information will be available and valid for the alarm fields of nvoOLCStatus
The minimum percentage value for a dimmable lamp to remain illuminated
The time it takes the lamp to warm-up to the full operating temperature
The maximum voltage (of the 1-10V output) needed to achieve 100% lamp-light output. In many cases, 100% is reached before the 10V-limit is reached
This configuration property sets the limits for nvoOLCStatus
This configuration property is be used to define the default output value on power up
GPS location where the physical device is located. This configuration property is mandatory if the SFPToutdoorLuminairController profile is implemented.
Load the program specified in SCPTprogSelect
Run the currently loaded program. If the program was halted manually, this will resume running from the point is was halted.
Halt the currently loaded program. This will preserve the program state and a subsequent Run command will resume the program from where it was halted.
Restart the currently loaded program from the beginning.

Executes the next logical operation (line, statement, instruction, logic block, etc.) of the currently loaded program. The program state must be "idle" or "halted" to accept this command, otherwise it will be ignored. The program returns to "halted" state after execution of this command.
A Static Programmable Device is used for flexible solution programming in an Open Systems installation to support different application cases
This input network variable is used to indicate the current status of the Program object.
The Program object may implement one or more network variables as input to the currently running program in the Program object.
The Program object may implement one or more network variables as output to the currently running program in the Program object.
This configuration property specifies the human-readable name of the currently loaded program.
This configuration property specifies the revision number of the currently loaded program.
This configuration property specifies which program is selected to be loaded, if the device can store multiple programs.
This configuration property specifies the location from where the source file was downloaded to the device.
This configuration property may be implemented if the program data is stored in one or more files on the device.
This configuration property contains a read-only list of the most recent commands to the device, along with a time stamp for each.
This configuration property contains a read-only list of the most recent state of the device, along with a time stamp for each.
Indicates the current status info for the Program object, including state, runtime total, and error code with time stamp.
Revision number and date of currently loaded program
Buffer Id where the currently loaded program is stored
Location from where the current program was downloaded
Indexes of first and last Lonmark files where programs may be stored
Log of recent commands, with time stamp
Log of recent status values, with time stamp
Loading (program is being loaded - will become Idle when done)
Running (may be halted by user, or halted if error occurs)
Halted (program has stopped due to an error or user command)
Unloading (program is being unloaded - will become "No Program" when done)

Executes the next logical operation (line, statement, instruction, logic block, etc.) of the currently loaded program. The program state must be "idle" or "halted" to accept this command, otherwise it will be ignored. The program returns to "halted" state after execution of this command
Program fault (resulted in a program halt)

Invalid operation (resulted in a program halt)
Enables current sensing for manual load control
Time period used for a measurement
Bit masks to specify membership in up to 64 ISI lighting groups. Group 0 is not used.
The nvoEnergyHi reading is incremented and the nvoEnergyLo reading wraps to zero when 1 kWh is reached for the nvoEnergyLo value. May be an estimated value.
Time period used for a power measurement; may be used to calibrate a power or energy sensor
This parameter is used to define the behavior when an override request is received via the Node Object.

The value to be used for the input when the functional block is overriden, and the behavior is "SPECIFIED"; if there is no Node object, a non-invalid value for the cpOvrValue state field triggers the override to the specified value, and the normal value is restored when the cpOvrValue state field changes to the invalid value
Offset to be applied to a load control value during standby mode
Offset to be applied to a load control value during demand-response mode
Offsets to be used during standby (unoccupied state but home, or sleep mode) and demand-response modes
The Functional Block index within the Node Self-Documentation string to which this program applies
Log of recent errors, with time stamp
This output network variable reports the Solar Global Radiation with W/m2

Typically Global Solar Radiation sensor object output is connected to the input of a controller. The Global Solar Radiation value can e.g. be used to estimate the energy input into a building by the sun.
The SCPTnvUsage CPs shall be used to indicate whether the NVs are in use by the loaded program
