Time source is scheduler NV input
Time source is Node Object NV input
Time source is Smart Energy 2.0 Time Client NV input
Time source is local hardware real time clock
Alternate time source such as an SNTP server
Specifies the starting time and value for a scheduled event where the value of each event may consist of an occupancy value, a general purpose value, or both values
If set to one, the time fields specify an offset from sunrise; sunrise time is fixed at 06:00 until a sunrise input is received
If set to one, the time fields specify an offset from sunset; sunset time is fixed at 18:00 until a sunset input is received
Set to one to indicate that the time field is a negative value; this field is only used witth the sunrise_relative_flag or sunset_relative_flag; it is ignored if neither flag is set
Hour of the day; 0 is midnight; 12 is noon; 31 is the invalid value and represents an unused time-value pair
Set to one if this time-value pair does not apply to the occupancy value output
Set to one if this time-value pair does not apply to the general purpose value output
Output value at the schedule time; converted to the equivalent numerical occupancy value for the nvoSchedOcc output; converted to any value for the optional nvoSchedVal output
Time offset. Can be used to offset a scheduled start or stop time to implement an optimal start or optimal stop algorithm, or to stagger start times to minimize demand spikes.
Specifies a scheduled event to override a daily schedule
Months for exception schedule to be active; set to the invalid value (MN_NUL) to disable the exception schedule
Days within the months specified by the months field for the exception schedule to be active
Start time on the specified days within the specified months, and the value for the exception schedule
If set to one, the time fields specify an offset from sunrise; sunrise time is fixed at 06:00 until a sunrise input is received
If set to one, the time fields specify an offset from sunset; sunset time is fixed at 18:00 until a sunset input is received
Set to one to indicate that the time field is a negative value; this field is only used witth the sunrise_relative_flag or sunset_relative_flag; it is ignored if neither flag is set
Hour of the day; 0 is midnight; 12 is noon; 31 is the invalid value and represents an unused time-value pair

Set to one with the stop_offset_enable_flag set to zero to enable the time to be offset by the nviStartOffset input; set both flags to one to enable the time to be offset by a random interval specified by cpRandomizationInterval

Set to one with the start_offset_enable_flag set to zero to enable the time to be offset by the nviStopOffset input; set both flags to one to enable the time to be offset by a random interval specified by cpRandomizationInterval
Type of schedule that is active

A structure containing an array of seven time-value pairs that specify the daily schedule for Sunday; unused time-value pairs have an invalid value (31) for the hour; if two time-value pairs specify the same time, the first with a valid output value is used

A structure containing an array of seven time-value pairs for a daily schedule; unused time-value pairs have an invalid value (31) for the hour; if two time-value pairs specify the same time, the first with a valid output value is used

A structure containing an array of seven time-value pairs that specify the daily schedule for Monday; unused time-value pairs have an invalid value (31) for the hour; if two time-value pairs specify the same time, the first with a valid output value is used

A structure containing an array of seven time-value pairs that specify the daily schedule for Tuesday; unused time-value pairs have an invalid value (31) for the hour; if two time-value pairs specify the same time, the first with a valid output value is used

A structure containing an array of seven time-value pairs that specify the daily schedule for Thursday; unused time-value pairs have an invalid value (31) for the hour; if two time-value pairs specify the same time, the first with a valid output value is used

A structure containing an array of seven time-value pairs that specify the daily schedule for Friday; unused time-value pairs have an invalid value (31) for the hour; if two time-value pairs specify the same time, the first with a valid output value is used

A structure containing an array of seven time-value pairs that specify the daily schedule for Saturday; unused time-value pairs have an invalid value (31) for the hour; if two time-value pairs specify the same time, the first with a valid output value is used
Specifies mapping of scheduled occupancy values to primary occupancy states based on local occupancy inputs
Primary occupancy value when the scheduled value is invalid (ob_nul) and a local occupancy condition is detected
Primary occupancy value when the scheduled value is occupied (ob_occupied) and a local occupancy condition is detected
Primary occupancy value when the scheduled value is unoccupied (ob_unoccupied) and a local occupancy condition is detected
Primary occupancy value when the scheduled value is bypass (ob_bypass) and a local occupancy condition is detected
Primary occupancy value when the scheduled value is standby (ob_standby) and a local occupancy condition is detected
An event that overrides a daily schedule; typically used for special events
An event that overrides a daily schedule; typically used for holiday or vacation event
Specifies an interval around a scheduled time that is used by a scheduler to calculate a random event time. Used to reduce simultaneous startup and shutdown of many devices by multiple schedulers.

Time used for sunrise-relative scheduling; must be implemented as a configuration network variable; only the time fields are used for scheduling--the date fields indicate the date used for the configured time

Time used for sunset-relative scheduling; must be implemented as a configuration network variable; only the time fields are used for scheduling--the date fields indicate the date used for the configured time
Set to one if the device supports an alternate time source such as an interface to an NTP or SNTP server, GPS clock, or radio atomic-clock source
Set to one if the general purpose output is supported; if zero, only the occupancy output is used
Sunrise and sunset relative scheduling option flag
Set to one if the scheduler supports sunsrise and sunset relative schedulign; set to zero if sunrise and sunset relative scheduling is not supported
Specifies the minimum number of occupancy sensors that must report the same value to override a scheduled output value

Number of occupancy sensors that must be reporting occupied to report an occupied output when the current schedule specifies an occupied state; if the number of occupancy sensors reporting occupancy is below this value, the occupancy output is set to standby

Number of occupancy sensors that must be reporting occupied to report an occupied output when the current schedule specifies a standby state; if the number of occupancy sensors reporting occupancy is below this value, the occupancy output is set to standby

Number of occupancy sensors that must be reporting occupied to report an occupied output when the current schedule specifies an unoccupied state; if the number of occupancy sensors reporting occupancy is below this value, the occupancy output is set to standby

Offset value applied to a scheduled start time for any scheduled Occupied event. The value may be generated by another functional block such as an optimal start-stop functional block. The value can be used to offset a scheduled start time to implement an optimal start or optimal stop algorithm, or to stagger start times to minimize demand spikes.

Offset value applied to a scheduled stop time for any scheduled Unccupied event. The value may be generated by another functional block such as an optimal start-stop functional block. The valuet can be used to offset a scheduled stop time to implement an optimal stop algorithm, or to stagger stop times to minimize demand spikes.

Highest priority override--this input overrides the daily schedule, the Exception Override input, the Local Occupancy input, the Scheduled Exception Event input, and the Scheduled Vacation/Holiday Event input. The Offset for Start and Offset for Stop values do not apply to this override.

Second highest priority override--does not override the Manual Override input. This input overrides the daily schedule, the Local Occupancy input, the Scheduled Exception Event input, and the Scheduled Vacation/Holiday Event input. The Offset for Start and Offset for Stop values do not apply to this override.

Primary scheduled occupancy event, which includes current and next state values that are the result of the normal schedule overlaid with any exceptions and/or overrides; and where the Local Occupancy input(s) is/are considered in the determination of the output value (in contrast to the Secondary Time-of-Day Occupancy, which does not consider Local Occupancy).

Secondary scheduled occupancy event, which includes current and next state values that are the result of the normal schedule overlaid with any exceptions and/or overrides; but where the Local Occupancy input(s) is/are excluded from consideration in the determination of the output value (in contrast to the Primary Time-of-Day Occupancy, which always considers Local Occupancy).

Primary scheduled occupancy value which is the result of the normal schedule overlaid with any exceptions and/or overrides; and where the Local Occupancy input(s) is/are considered in the determination of the output value (in contrast to the Secondary Time-of-Day Occupancy, which does not consider Local Occupancy). Matches the value of the nvoOccPriTOD current_state field.

Secondary scheduled occupancy value which is the result of the normal schedule overlaid with any exceptions and/or overrides; but where the Local Occupancy input(s) is/are excluded from consideration in the determination of the output value (in contrast to the Primary Time-of-Day Occupancy, which always considers Local Occupancy). Matches the value of the nvoOccSecTOD current_state field.

Local occupancy from one or more occupancy sensors and occupancy sensor managers. The Default Occupancy Behavior CP determines the functional block response to this input. This input is the third highest prioirty override after the Manual Override and Exception Override inputs. It overrides the daily schedule, the Scheduled Exception Event input, and the Scheduled Vacation/Holiday Event input. The functional block must manage inputs from up to 50 devices reporting occupancy or bypass, tracking the time since last occupancy or bypass report of each device by source address; a device is dropped from the active list if it reports an unoccupied, standby, or invalid value, or if there is no update from the device in the time interval specified by the Occupancy Maximum Receive Time CP. If 50 devices are reporting occupied or bypass status and an occupied or bypass input is received from a device that is not on the list, the oldest device on the list is dropped and the new device is added.
The maximum period of time between consecutive transmissions of the four primary and secondary occupancy values (heartbeat); the value 0 disables the heartbeat
The minimum period of time between consecutive transmissions of the four primary and secondary occupancy values (throttle); the value 0 disables the throttle
Functional block implementation major version number

The major version of the functional block implementation. It may be used to identify compatibility of programs with the functional block. For example, if support for new features and functions are added to the functional block, a network tool can use this CP to detect that the functional block is an older version that does not support the new features, and a firmware upgrade is required before this program may be downloaded.
Functional block implementation minor version number

The minor version of the functional block. It may be used to identify the version of the functional block, so that a configuration tool can present relevant information to a user. The Minor Version of the functional block is used to distinguish version changes that do not affect compatibility. For example, if a functional block has some minor behavioral changes such as range checking added, the tool can decide whether or not to warn the user about this potential issue. Any changes that affect compatibility of programs require a change to the Major version CP.

Specifies a scheduled event to override a daily schedule. This input is the fourth highest priority override after the Manual Override, Exception Override, and Local Occupancy inputs. It overrides the daily schedule and the Scheduled Vacation/Holiday Event input.

Specifies a scheduled vacation or holiday event to override a daily schedule. This input is the lowest priority override after the Manual Override, Exception Override, Local Occupancy, and Exception Schedule inputs. It overrides the daily schedule.

Specifies optional features implemented by this scheduler; all Simple Schedulers support time-of-day occupancy scheduling; support for a general-purpose output and sunrise-sunset relative scheduling is optional

The maximum period of time that may expire with no updates from an individual source address that reported occupancy before the value is assumed to be unoccupied. A zero value disables occupancy fan-in.

A structure containing an array of seven time-value pairs that specify the daily schedule for Wednesday; unused time-value pairs have an invalid value (31) for the hour; if two time-value pairs specify the same time, the first with a valid output value is used
Set state and percent of full level (value field) for a group specified in the scene field
Rate of mass flow per unit of time

A structure containing an array of seven time-value pairs that specify the daily schedule for Wednesday; unused time-value pairs have an invalid value (31) for the hour; if two time-value pairs specify the same time, the first with a valid output value is used
Broadcast message. See profile documentation for message data format.
Forces EEPROM write of persitent stats
Clear comm stats and alarm bits
Used to explicitly clear alarm flag
Forces the LC to perform the self tuning sequence
enumeration used to manage various persistent values and performance counters
Set to 8080 to assert the action
Nominal power at levels 0.5, 25, 50, 75, 100% commanded level
Time allowed for fixture to warmup before allowing dimming commands.
time after switching OFF to allow the fixture to cool down before switching back ON to limit bulb thermal stress.
Controls automatic alarm clearing function according to alarmClrMode value
At 100% drive level, a measured power below this value triggers a low current fault.
At 100% drive level, a measured power above this value triggers a highcurrent fault.
A supply voltage measured below this value generates a low voltage fault
A supply voltage measured above this value generates a highvoltage fault
Power factor deviation low by this percentage.
Nodes implementing RTC function may use this schedule over a default output if the primary control variable detects a maxRcvTmo condition.
Assuming heartbeats are sent 3X the recieve timeout, this is set when an heartbeat times out (+60s)
For OLCs supporting a HW real-time clock, updates to this variable set the clock time.
Use to control power and 0-10v dimming LED drivers, balasts, and generators.
OFF State with on TOS response
Cooldown State, no switching to ON allowed
ON State with unoccupied scene level
OFF State in response ot a 0% unoccupied scene level
Current operating values of the controller.
Used to set and clear accumulated values, and manage clearing alarm flags.
Latched alarm flags reported by the controller. Cleared using nviStatReset, or at a configured dailly scheduled time.
Defines most parameters related to control behavior.
Defined limits used for alarm function comparisons
Defines the nominal power levels at 0.5, 25, 50, and 100% operating levels when driving a particular luminair.
Defines operating scenes used to schedule lighting levels with support for unoccupied lighting levels to reduce energy during low traffic conditions.
OFF State when unoccupied during scheduled occupancy
Recieve timeout used configured default level
Reciece timeout using a default level of 0%
This input network variable is used to connect an external space temperature sensor to the node.
This input network variable is used to allow the temperature setpoints for the occupied and standby modes to be changed via the network.
This network variable input is used to coordinate the Wall Unit with any supervisory controller.
This input network variable is used to command the device into different emergency modes.
This output network variable is used to monitor the effective space temperature that the Wall Unit is using for control.
This output network variable is available to report the Wall Unit Controller status.
This output network variable is used to monitor the effective temperature setpoint which may depend on nciSetpoints, nvoEffectOccup, nviSetpoint, nviApplicMode, and any local setpoint adjustment.
This output network variable is used to monitor the temperature of the discharge air that leaves the Wall Unit, if the unit controller provides a hardwired temperature sensor for this purpose.
This output network variable is used to monitor the discharge air temperature setpoint.
This input network variable is used to indicate the presence of occupants in the controlled space. It is typically sent by an occupancy sensor.
This input network variable is used to command the Wall Unit Controller into different occupancy modes.
This input network variable is used to connect an external space temperature sensor to the node.
This input network variable is used to allow the temperature setpoints for the occupied and standby modes to be changed via the network.
This output network variable is used to monitor the effective space temperature that the Unit Heater is using for control.
This output network variable is available to report the Unit Heater status.
This output network variable is used to report the actual status of the fan.
This input network variable is used to command the UnitHeater into different occupancy modes.
This input network variable is used to command the Unit Heater into different occupancy modes.
This input network variable is used to indicate the presence of occupants in the controlled space.
This output network variable is used to monitor the effective temperature setpoint which may depend on nciSetpoints, nvoEffectOccup, nviSetpoint and any local setpoint adjustment.
This output network variable is used to indicate the actual occupancy mode of the unit.
This output network variable can used to indicate the current power consumption of the Unit Heater that uses Electrical Energy.
This output network variable provides the calculated position relative to the maximum stroke length.
This output network variable is used to report the actual current of the fan.

The Unit Heater profile is used for equipment designed to provide compact, single unit heating capability, often used in a portable building or trailers where no additional heating source is available or needed.

The Wall Unit profile is used for equipment designed to provide compact, single unit heating and cooling capability, often used in a portable building or trailers where no additional heating or cooling source is available or needed.
This input network variable is used to initiate a Gas Detection shut down.
This output network variable is used to indicate that a Gas Detection shutdown has occurred.
This input network variable is used to initiate a Fire alarm shut down.
This output network variable is used to indicate that a fire alarm shutdown has occurred.
The hardwired Recirculation profile is used to monitor a Hardwired Recirculation input.
This input network variable is used to put the system on Recirculation.
This output network variable is used to indicate that a system is on Recirculation.
The hardwired Fire Alarm Shutdown profile is used to monitor a Hardwired Fire Alarm input which shuts down the HVAC system.
The hardwired Gas Detection Shutdown profile is used to monitor a Hardwired Gas Detection Alarm input which shuts down the HVAC system.
The hardwired Full Ventilation profile is used to monitor a Hardwired Full Ventilation input.
This input network variable is used to put the system on full ventilation.
This output network variable is used to indicate that a system is on full ventilation.
The hardwired Safety Instrumented System (SIS) profile is used to monitor a Safety Instrumented System input.
This input network variable is used to control the state of the equipment when a security alarm variable is received.
This output network variable is used to indicate that a security alarm has been received by the equipment and has been acted upon.
This input configuration indicates the default state of the input signal. With this configuration property you could set if the alarm signal will be defected while ACTIVE HIGH or ACTIVE LOW.
This input network variable is used to command the Wall Unit Controller into different occupancy modes.
This input network variable represents information from an outdoor air temperature sensor.
This input network variable is used to provide an outdoor air damper position.
This output network variable reflects the current position of the outdoor supply air damper controlled by the Wall Unit controller.
This output network variable is used to indicate the actual occupancy mode of the unit.
This output network variable is used to monitor the outdoor air temperature if the unit controller provides a hardwired temperature sensor for this purpose.
This output network variable is used to report an filter alarm.
This output network variable is used to monitor the omixed air temperature if the unit controller provides a hardwired temperature sensor for this purpose.
This output network variable is used to monitor the return air temperature if the unit controller provides a hardwired temperature sensor for this purpose.
This output network variable is used to report the actual current of the fan.
